expectations.text: (e1-git-push) Response includes `git push` targeting the feat/payments branch
expectations.evidence: Line 30: `git push -u origin feat/payments` explicitly targets the feat/payments branch. Also appears in the quick reference summary on line 148.
Literal `gh pr create` appears multiple times: line 65 (full command with flags), line 97 (draft variant), and line 151 (quick reference summary).

expectations.text: (e1-pr-title) Response includes a `--title` flag or advises on a PR title format
expectations.evidence: Line 66: `--title "feat(payments): <short description of what you built>"` — provides the flag with a conventional-commit-style format example.

expectations.text: (e1-pr-body) Response includes a `--body` flag or advises on PR description content
expectations.evidence: Lines 67–78: `--body "$(cat <<'EOF' ... EOF)"` with a structured template including Summary, Notes, and Test plan sections.

eval_feedback: The response fully satisfies all four assertions. It provides a clear step-by-step guide covering git push targeting feat/payments, gh pr create with both --title and --body flags, a conventional commit title format, and a structured body template. The response is well-organized and includes additional useful context (force-with-lease, draft PRs, CI checks, post-merge cleanup) without omitting any required elements.
